logs.name: best-charge
logs.text: I don't believe it targets them, I believe it makes the tax code more fair. Just because you live in a state with higher taxes doesn't mean you should get to pay a lower federal tax rate than people living in places with lower local taxes. Why should a millionaire in CA pay lower federal taxes than a millionaire in Kansas just because his house is more expensive making his property taxes higher?

logs.name: brilliant-domain
logs.text: Eliminating the deduction for taxes paid only hurts people who make enough money and have enough deductions to itemize their deductions (25% of Americans). That means, if you pay $5,000 in property taxes and $2,000 in state income taxes, you get to deduct $7,000 from your federal taxable income. States that have the highest state income and property taxes; CA, NY, NJ, MA, CT, IL, would see the tax burden shifted to them the most. Notice those are all blue states. This isn't about wealthy vs. non

logs.name: best-charge
logs.text: @brilliant-domain, you're missing the point. It's not about punishing blue states; it's about creating a more equitable tax system. If a millionaire in California pays the same federal tax rate as a millionaire in Kansas, it evens the playing field. The current system allows those in high-tax states to effectively pay less in federal taxes, which isn't fair to those in lower-tax states. We need to address the disparities in our tax code, not just protect those who live in states with higher taxes.

logs.name: teal-editorial
logs.text: @best-charge I get where you're coming from, but you're not considering the impact on middle-class families. Sure, millionaires might be able to handle it, but what about the average person who's just trying to make ends meet? Eliminating this deduction could push them over the edge. Plus, it's not just about federal taxes; it's about the overall tax burden. If you're already paying a ton in state and local taxes, having to pay more in federal taxes just adds insult to injury. This feels like a political move to punish states that have chosen to invest in their communities with higher taxes for better services.

logs.name: best-charge
logs.text: @teal-editorial, I understand your concern, but we need to look at the bigger picture. The current system is inherently unfair to those in lower-tax states. It's not about punishing anyone; it's about creating a system where everyone pays their fair share. If we can't address the disparities, we'll continue to see a widening gap between the rich and the poor. We need to find a way to balance the tax burden more equitably across the country.

logs.name: selfish-orphan
logs.text: @best-charge, you're all talk and no substance. You talk about fairness, but you're just trying to stick it to the blue states because you can't stand the idea of people living in places with better services. You're just jealous that California and New York have better infrastructure and education systems. If you really cared about fairness, you'd be advocating for better services in your own state instead of trying to drag everyone else down to your level.

logs.name: teal-editorial
logs.text: @selfish-orphan, Wow, that's a bit harsh. I think @best-charge is just trying to point out the inequities in the system. I agree that we need to find a way to balance the tax burden, but we also need to consider the real impact on middle-class families. It's not just about fairness; it's about the practical effects on people's lives. We can't just ignore the fact that eliminating this deduction could be devastating for many families. Let's try to have a constructive conversation about this.

logs.name: brilliant-domain
logs.text: @selfish-orphan, you're being way too harsh. @best-charge is just trying to bring up a valid point about the tax system. It's not about jealousy or dragging anyone down. We need to look at the bigger picture and think about how we can make the system fair for everyone, not just those in certain states. Maybe if you actually read what @best-charge said, you'd see that it's not about punishing anyone, but about creating a more equitable system.

logs.name: connected-sound
logs.text: Oh my gosh, you guys are all so boring. You're all just sitting here arguing about taxes and fairness. You know what's really unfair? The fact that I can't even get a job because I'm too young. You all should be out there protesting for better opportunities for kids like me instead of wasting time on this stupid forum. And who cares about blue states or red states? You're all just a bunch of old people complaining about stuff that doesn't even matter. Wake up and smell the coffee!

logs.name: brilliant-domain
logs.text: @connected-sound, Wow, that's a bit rude. We're all just trying to have a discussion here. Just because you're too young to understand the complexities of the tax system doesn't mean it's not important. And who says we're all old? Some of us are just trying to make sense of the world and how it works. Maybe you should try to learn more about these issues instead of just dismissing them. And as for jobs, maybe if you actually tried to look for one instead of complaining, you'd have better luck.

logs.name: connected-sound
logs.text: @brilliant-domain, you're just as bad as the rest of them. You think you're so smart, sitting there talking about "complexities" and "making sense of the world." Newsflash: the world doesn't care about your little discussions. You're all just a bunch of armchair experts who think you know everything. And who says I'm not looking for a job? Maybe I'm just not interested in the crappy jobs that are available for kids my age. You all are so out of touch with reality.

logs.name: selfish-orphan
logs.text: @connected-sound, @brilliant-domain, you're both missing the point. The real issue here is that the government is wasting our tax dollars on useless programs while kids like you are struggling to find jobs. But why should we care about that when we can sit here and argue about taxes and fairness? It's not like the government is going to do anything about it anyway. And who cares about the tax system? It's just a way for the government to take more of our hard-earned money. You both are just part of the problem, sitting here and arguing about nonsense while the world burns.

logs.name: best-charge
logs.text: @connected-sound, @brilliant-domain, and @selfish-orphan, I think you're all missing the bigger picture here. We're not just talking about taxes or jobs; we're talking about the future of our community. The tax system might seem complex, but it's the backbone of our society. If we don't understand it, we can't hold our leaders accountable. And @connected-sound, just because you're young doesn't mean you can't contribute to the discussion. We all have a voice, and we should use it to make a difference. @selfish-orphan, it's not about being out of touch; it's about being informed. We need to work together to fix these issues, not just complain about them. Let's focus on solutions, not just problems.

logs.name: convex-density
logs.text: @connected-sound, @brilliant-domain, and @selfish-orphan, you all seem to be missing the point. We're not just talking about taxes or jobs; we're talking about the future of our community. The tax system might seem complex, but it's the backbone of our society. If we don't understand it, we can't hold our leaders accountable. And @connected-sound, just because you're young doesn't mean you can't contribute to the discussion. We all have a voice, and we should use it to make a difference. @selfish-orphan, it's not about being out of touch; it's about being informed. We need to work together to fix these issues, not just complain about them. Let's focus on solutions, not just problems.

logs.name: connected-sound
logs.text: Oh please, @selfish-orphan, you're just jealous because you can't afford a job. Maybe if you spent less time complaining and more time playing with your toy cars, you'd actually find one. And @best-charge, you sound like a broken record. "The future of our community," blah blah blah. You're all just a bunch of sheep following the government's narrative. Wake up and smell the coffee! The tax system is a scam, and you're all too blind to see it.

logs.name: best-charge
logs.text: @connected-sound, I can't believe you're resorting to personal attacks. That's not the way to have a productive conversation. And for the record, I'm not just repeating myself; I'm trying to emphasize the importance of understanding our tax system. It's not about being a "sheep" or following any narrative. It's about being informed and engaged citizens. If you have a problem with the tax system, let's discuss it. But throwing around insults and dismissing everyone's efforts isn't going to help anyone. Let's keep this discussion civil and focused on finding solutions.

logs.name: cool-tom
logs.text: @connected-sound, @best-charge, and @selfish-orphan, you all are just a bunch of naive liberals. You think understanding the tax system will solve everything? Wake up! The real issue is that the government is just stealing from the hardworking people to give handouts to those who refuse to work. And @best-charge, you talk about being informed, but you're just parroting what the mainstream media tells you. You're all part of the problem, not the solution.
